newsBriefing: Today's top headlines are driven by roster churn, contract maneuvering and one global superstar performance. In the NFL, the Chargers agreed with center Tyler Biadasz, the Rams re-signed safety Kam Curl on a three-year deal, the Browns reworked Deshaun Watson's contract to trim his massive cap hit, and the Raiders are expected to release Geno Smith unless a late trade materializes. In the NHL, Minnesota added forward Bobby Brink from Philadelphia, adding to the trade-deadline reshaping among playoff hopefuls. On the international baseball stage, Shohei Ohtani blasted a grand slam as Japan routed Chinese Taipei in the WBC, reinforcing Japan's title-favorite profile. Off the field, the Suns' Dillon Brooks was arrested on suspicion of DUI in Arizona, and in MMA, Francis Ngannou and the PFL officially parted ways, making the former heavyweight champion one of combat sports' biggest free agents.
